message:
fix(runtime): MULTI transaction queueing and CONFIG SET atomicity

- SELECT, PUBLISH, SWAPDB, CONFIG, ACL, CLIENT, and other runtime-special
  commands are now properly queued inside MULTI (matching Redis behavior)
  instead of executing immediately. Only MULTI/EXEC/DISCARD/WATCH/UNWATCH/
  QUIT/RESET bypass queueing.
- SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE family rejected inside MULTI with proper error.
- CONFIG SET is now atomic: notify-keyspace-events and list-max-listpack-size
  are deferred until all parameters validate, preventing partial application
  on error.
- Fix CONFIG GET appenddirname to return directory name only (not full path).
- Lua pcall/xpcall: replace insert(0) with pre-allocated push+extend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
